plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index exposure.
Add XLK as a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation and significantly reduced risk. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also limited upside potential.

moves.note: Press what is working, strong P&L

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely and trim SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to rebalance and address its trim flag.
Add to CRWD and SMH, pressing into strong current performance and scanner setups.
Introduce XLK as a new quality tech holding.
This plan balances risk and growth with a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, aiming for growth while managing risk. It blends broad index exposure with quality tech and a small amount of leverage. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

moves.note: Press what is working, strong P&L

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop.
Trim QQQI to address its trim flag and rebalance.
Increase CRWD and SMH, leaning into strong performance and top scanner setups.
Introduce XLK and META as new high-scoring tech buys.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretch growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. This approach significantly amplifies both potential gains and drawdowns, with leveraged ETFs subject to decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leverage and high-beta single names.
Significantly increase QQQI to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
Increase QQQ exposure for broad index stability.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield with a low drawdown.
Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It aims to park cash for yield, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. Expect very low volatility but limited growth potential.
